title: Jackiw-Rebbi Zero Mode for a Sign-Changing Dirac Mass
summary: If a Dirac mass profile changes sign across an interface, the Dirac operator admits a normalizable bound state whose envelope is the exponential of minus the mass integral.
assumptions: The asymptotic masses at x_1 -> plus/minus infinity have opposite sign.
The tangential momentum is set to the zero-mode value when solving the transverse localization problem.
regime: Continuum Dirac operator with a one-dimensional sign-changing mass profile.
scope: Reusable theorem behind the domain-wall derivation of chiral boundary modes in Lecture Three.

source_anchors.notes: Canonical source for the interface zero-mode mechanism.
Witten recasts the doubled Hall edge problem as a Jackiw-Rebbi domain-wall solution.

math_expressions.context: Dirac equation with a sign-changing mass profile.
Normalizable zero-mode solution localized near the domain wall.

symbols.meaning: mass profile whose sign changes across the interface

failure_modes: If the mass does not change sign there is no normalizable interface zero mode of this form.
retrieval_hints: Use when reconstructing the detailed boundary-mode derivation instead of citing Jackiw-Rebbi by name only.
